title: Your QR Code Might Be a Liar. Ours IsnГўв‚¬в„ўt. How we combat QR Code Hijacking!
author: Jonathan Bush
excerpt: Scammers are hijacking QR codes in broad daylight and your front desk might be ground zero. If your code gets hijacked one more time, itГўв‚¬в„ўs going to need trauma counseling. DialZero shuts it down with instant verificationГўв‚¬вЂќand replaces the mess of scattered codes with one secure QR that holds everything your guests need.
body:
You ever scan a QR code expecting a dinner menu and suddenly you're three clicks deep into a crypto scam and your phoneГўв‚¬в„ўs asking if it should download something? Yeah, same.

Welcome to the wild west of QR code hijacking, where scammers slap fake codes over real ones and ride off into the digital sunset with your data. And in the hospitality world, where QR codes are everywhereГўв‚¬вЂќroom service menus, Wi-Fi logins, booking linksГўв‚¬вЂќthatГўв‚¬в„ўs more than just annoying. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs a security nightmare.

At DialZero, weГўв‚¬в„ўre putting an end to the chaos.

When a guest scans a DialZero QR code, they get instant verification confirming theyГўв‚¬в„ўre in the right place. No shady redirects. No mystery links. Just exactly what the hotel wants them to see.

And hereГўв‚¬в„ўs the twist: Instead of a dozen QR codes taped to the check-in desk like itГўв‚¬в„ўs a tech-themed vision board, we give hotels one secure QR code that does it all. Menus? Yes. Spa booking? Yep. Feedback forms, Wi-Fi, digital concierge, room upgrades? All there. One code. Zero nonsense.

Scam-proof. Guest-friendly. Manager-approved.

Because the only thing guests should be stealing from your hotel is the shampoo. Well hopefully not but let's be realistic!
